solute.description: This molecule is a derivative of phenylalanine, consisting of a propanoic acid backbone with an alpha-amino group and a 2,4-dinitrophenyl substituent at the beta position. The aromatic ring is heavily substituted with two strong electron-withdrawing nitro groups located at the *ortho* and *para* positions relative to the alkyl chain. It is a medium-sized, highly polar molecule with significant hydrogen bonding capacity provided by the carboxylic acid, primary amine, and the oxygen atoms of the nitro groups. The structure is distinctive for its combination of a standard amino acid scaffold with a highly deactivated, dinitro-substituted aromatic system.

solvent.description: This molecule consists of a short, two-carbon saturated chain (ethane backbone) with no branching or rings. It features two chlorine atoms covalently bonded to different carbon atoms in a vicinal (1,2) arrangement. As a small halogenated hydrocarbon, it possesses moderate polarity due to the C-Cl dipoles but lacks any hydrogen bond donors or acceptors. Its distinctive character arises from the symmetrical placement of electronegative substituents on a flexible ethylene bridge, allowing for distinct gauche and anti conformational isomers.
